page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance optimized for network and EBS performance. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration features 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on a high-speed 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage and network communication.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in memory-intensive and high-frequency compute tasks. The server achieved a top-tier rating in the Passmark memory score, supported by strong large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths. Single-threaded gzip compression and software compilation using clang also demonstrated strong performance, placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Standard CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, fell within the average range. However, the system exhibits weak to poor performance in LLM text generation tasks, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

As a bare-metal instance with dedicated high-frequency compute resources and 100 Gbps networking, the m5zn.metal offers high resource density and direct hardware access. This makes it qualitatively efficient for workloads requiring high single-core performance and high network throughput without virtualization overhead. It is well-aligned with workloads such as software compilation, network-intensive applications, database operations, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring a dedicated 24-core Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 48 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 100 Gbps baseline network interface, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier memory performance and strong software compilation capabilities, alongside average multi-core processing speeds. The instance is designed for workloads requiring direct hardware access, high-frequency single-core execution, and high network throughput, such as software compilation, database operations, and network-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.metal is a bare-metal server featuring a 48-vCPU Intel Xeon processor at 4.5 GHz, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-frequency compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.metal is a bare-metal server with 48 vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps networking for high-frequency workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal instance featuring dedicated processing cores, top-tier memory performance, and high-speed network connectivity for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 48 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz.
192.0 GB of DDR4 system memory with top-tier benchmarked performance.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network-heavy tasks.
Bare-metal architecture providing direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead.
Strong performance in software compilation and large-block memory operations.
Suited for database operations, network-intensive applications, and high-frequency compute.
